meta.methodology: 5-query competition search per gap (direct competitors, OSS, first-party, top-N lists, funded startups)
meta.scoring_notes: reality_check_score = original_score + first_party_modifier + velocity_penalty + saturation_penalty + top_n_penalty. Negative modifiers reduce score.

reality_checks.gap_title: AI Agent Incident Response / Kill Switch

competitors_found.name: Runtime Fence / $KILLSWITCH
competitors_found.key_metric: Works with LangChain, AutoGPT, Copilot/Cursor/Aider; 100% offline

competitors_found.name: AgentGateway (Linux Foundation)
competitors_found.key_metric: Contributors from AWS, Cisco, Huawei, IBM, Microsoft, Red Hat, Shell

competitors_found.name: Zenity
competitors_found.key_metric: $59.5M raised; Gartner Cool Vendor 2025; 65 employees
competitors_found.backed_by: Third Point Ventures, DTCP, Intel Capital

competitors_found.name: ClawSight
competitors_found.key_metric: Live log streaming, cost/token tracking, remote kill switch

competitors_found.name: AgentGuard
competitors_found.key_metric: Auto-kills agent process on budget limit

competitors_found.name: Aegis
competitors_found.key_metric: Cryptographic audit trail, human-in-the-loop, kill switch, zero code changes

competitors_found.name: AutoGuard (ICLR 2026 paper)
competitors_found.key_metric: DOM-embedded defensive prompts to trigger LLM safety mechanisms

competitors_found.name: CrowdStrike Falcon (agentic security)
competitors_found.key_metric: AI-native tools protecting entire AI ecosystem

competitors_found.name: Palo Alto Networks (AI Kill Switch podcast/capability)
competitors_found.key_metric: Integrated into Protect AI acquisition

reality_checks.first_party_details: Microsoft added agent identities in Entra ID + guardrail controls in Microsoft Foundry (RSAC 2026 preview). Google ADK has multi-layered safety with Gemini-as-guardrail callbacks. Both are building graduated agent control primitives but not standalone kill-switch products.
reality_checks.top_n_source: reco.ai '8 Best AI Agent Security Tools for CISOs 2026'; fast.io 'Best AI Agent Security Tools 2026'
reality_checks.differentiation_angle: The generic kill-switch space is crowded. Only viable angle: enterprise-grade incident response ORCHESTRATION (not just a kill button) combining graduated response playbooks, cross-agent coordination, forensic replay, and compliance-grade audit trails. Pure kill switches are now commodity OSS.

competitors_found.name: Prem Studio
competitors_found.key_metric: Autonomous fine-tuning system; 30+ base models; intelligent model/method recommendation; free tier available

competitors_found.name: Hugging Face AutoTrain / Skills Training
competitors_found.key_metric: Automatic hardware matching, auto-selects LoRA for >3B models, cost optimization built-in

competitors_found.name: SiliconFlow
competitors_found.key_metric: Cheapest fine-tuning provider 2026; 2.3x faster inference, 32% lower latency vs competitors

competitors_found.name: Together AI Fine-Tuning
competitors_found.key_metric: UPipe cuts peak memory 82.5%; FFT optimizers reduce memory 25%

competitors_found.name: Unsloth
competitors_found.key_metric: 2x faster fine-tuning on single GPU; free on Colab/Kaggle

competitors_found.name: LlamaFactory
competitors_found.key_metric: 100+ LLMs/VLMs supported; ACL 2024 paper; 16-bit/LoRA/QLoRA

competitors_found.name: Oumi
competitors_found.key_metric: 10M to 405B parameters; SFT/LoRA/QLoRA/GRPO

competitors_found.name: FinetuneDB
competitors_found.key_metric: Dedicated AI fine-tuning platform

reality_checks.first_party_details: Azure OpenAI has built-in fine-tuning cost management with per-job billing caps ($5K), model selection guidance, and batch mode (50% off). OpenAI platform has similar. Google Vertex AI offers AutoML with cost-optimized training. All hyperscalers provide cost advisors within their fine-tuning services.
reality_checks.top_n_source: siliconflow.com 'Ultimate Guide - The Top and The Best Cheapest Model Fine-Tuning Providers of 2026'; labelyourdata.com 'LLM Fine-Tuning Tools: Best Picks 2026'
reality_checks.differentiation_angle: Prem Studio already does intelligent model/method recommendation. The only uncovered niche: a STANDALONE cost-optimization advisor that works ACROSS platforms (not locked to one vendor) and provides TCO projections including inference costs, not just training. Must be vendor-agnostic to differentiate from platform-native advisors.

reality_checks.gap_title: Edge/On-Device SLM Fine-Tuning and Deployment Platform

competitors_found.name: QVAC Fabric LLM (Tether Data)
competitors_found.key_metric: FIRST production-ready framework for fine-tuning on mobile GPUs (Qualcomm Adreno, ARM Mali); cross-platform (iOS/Android/desktop/server)

competitors_found.name: ExecuTorch (Meta)
competitors_found.key_metric: 12+ hardware backends (Apple, Qualcomm, Arm, MediaTek, Vulkan); used for Gemma-3-4B on Android

competitors_found.name: Google LiteRT-LM / MediaPipe LLM Inference
competitors_found.key_metric: LoRA fine-tuning support on attention layers; runs Gemma/Llama/Phi-4/Qwen on-device

competitors_found.name: Unsloth (mobile deployment)
competitors_found.key_metric: Deploy fine-tuned LLMs to iOS/Android phones

competitors_found.name: Ollama
competitors_found.key_metric: Democratized local AI deployment; production-ready for desktop/edge

competitors_found.name: Edge Impulse
competitors_found.key_metric: tinyML, sensor intelligence, embedded AI; full edge ML lifecycle

competitors_found.name: Liquid AI (LFM2.5)
competitors_found.key_metric: Next-gen on-device AI models optimized for edge

competitors_found.name: RunAnywhere
competitors_found.key_metric: On-device inference + enterprise control plane + analytics

competitors_found.name: MobiLLM (research)
competitors_found.key_metric: Enabling LLM fine-tuning on mobile devices

reality_checks.first_party_details: Google has LiteRT-LM with LoRA on-device fine-tuning + MediaPipe LLM Inference (iOS/Android). Apple Neural Engine supports on-device inference via CoreML. Qualcomm AI Engine (Hexagon/Adreno) provides INT8 inference + AIMET/ONNX. Meta ExecuTorch covers 12+ backends. All major chip vendors are building this capability natively.
reality_checks.top_n_source: runanywhere.ai 'Top Edge AI Solutions 2026'; siliconflow.com 'Best Small LLMs For Edge Devices 2026'
reality_checks.differentiation_angle: QVAC Fabric already delivers exactly this thesis (fine-tune -> quantize -> deploy on mobile GPUs). Google, Meta, Qualcomm all have production SDKs. The full lifecycle platform is no longer a gap - it is an active battleground among the largest tech companies. Any startup entering now faces first-party SDK competition from every chip vendor.

competitors_found.name: Cisco MCP Scanner
competitors_found.key_metric: 830 GitHub stars; 3 scanning engines (YARA, LLM-as-judge, Cisco AI Defense); CLI + REST API

competitors_found.name: Snyk Agent Scan
competitors_found.key_metric: Detects 15+ security risks; auto-discovers Claude/Cursor/Gemini/Windsurf configs; free self-service scanner

competitors_found.name: MCP-Shield
competitors_found.key_metric: Scans config files; works offline; zero dependencies; detects tool poisoning/exfiltration/cross-origin escalation

competitors_found.name: Proximity
competitors_found.key_metric: Scans MCP prompts/tools/resources for security risks

competitors_found.name: Enkrypt AI MCP Scan
competitors_found.key_metric: Automated agentic static analysis for command/path/prompt/code injection

competitors_found.name: MCP Guard
competitors_found.key_metric: Static analysis + dynamic testing + intelligent fuzzing

competitors_found.name: Sentinel
competitors_found.key_metric: Config + probe + container scanning for MCP

competitors_found.name: Golf Scanner
competitors_found.key_metric: Scans IDE/AI tool configs; 15 security checks; finds every MCP server

competitors_found.name: Aguara
competitors_found.key_metric: Go binary; static analysis on agent skill files and MCP servers

competitors_found.name: Sigil Security
competitors_found.key_metric: AI Agent Supply Chain Security Scanner (dedicated product)

competitors_found.name: MintMCP Gateway
competitors_found.key_metric: First SOC 2 Type II certified MCP platform; registry for approved servers

competitors_found.name: Qualys TotalAI (MCP coverage)
competitors_found.key_metric: MCP servers treated as shadow IT; enterprise-grade scanning

reality_checks.first_party_details: Anthropic has not shipped a first-party MCP security scanner, but their own MCP servers (Git, filesystem) have had critical CVEs found and patched. The MCP specification itself does not include built-in security scanning. Cisco and Snyk have filled the gap with corporate backing.
reality_checks.top_n_source: akto.io 'Top 10 MCP Security Tools 2025'; cm-alliance.com 'Top MCP Security Tools 2025'; adversa.ai 'Top MCP Security Resources March 2026'
reality_checks.differentiation_angle: This gap no longer exists. Cisco (corporate-backed OSS), Snyk ($900M+ funded), plus 10+ OSS scanners launched in the last 6 months. There are already 30+ CVEs cataloged, multiple top-N comparison articles, and Qualys/Palo Alto covering this in enterprise platforms. The window has closed. The only remaining micro-niche might be continuous registry monitoring (like a Snyk for MCP registries specifically), but Snyk itself is already building this.

reality_checks.gap_title: Compliance-Aware Training Pipeline for Regulated Industries

competitors_found.name: Collibra AI Governance
competitors_found.key_metric: Audit trails for regulated AI; EU AI Act/NIST templates; data lineage from source through training to deployment; integrates with Azure AI Foundry + MLflow

competitors_found.name: IBM watsonx.governance
competitors_found.key_metric: Enterprise AI governance across full lifecycle; risk management and compliance

competitors_found.name: DataRobot AI Governance
competitors_found.key_metric: Real-time AI governance; compliance policies/rules/alerts; asset discovery

competitors_found.name: ModelOp
competitors_found.key_metric: Centralized AI inventory as auditable system of record; risk tiering and compliance

competitors_found.name: OneTrust AI Governance
competitors_found.key_metric: Data governance frameworks; regulatory compliance; inventory/classify/control data usage

competitors_found.name: FairNow
competitors_found.key_metric: AI governance and GRC platform; integrates with MLOps and workflow tools

competitors_found.name: Weights & Biases (experiment tracking + audit)
competitors_found.key_metric: Version control for data/models; experiment tracking tied to audit trails

competitors_found.name: OpenLineage + Marquez
competitors_found.key_metric: Open standard for lineage metadata; integrates with Airflow/Spark/dbt

competitors_found.name: AWS AI Governance (SageMaker + Bedrock Guardrails)
competitors_found.key_metric: 140+ compliance certifications; HIPAA/FedRAMP/GDPR; model monitoring; GRC guide for financial services

competitors_found.name: Azure AI Foundry Governance
competitors_found.key_metric: Model cards, content safety, evaluation metrics; exportable PDF/SPDX for GRC audits

reality_checks.first_party_details: AWS SageMaker has compliance-integrated training pipelines with 140+ certifications. Azure AI Foundry exports model cards as PDF/SPDX for audit workflows. Both hyperscalers have published specific GRC guides for regulated industries (financial services, healthcare). These are not bolt-on features - they are core platform capabilities.
reality_checks.top_n_source: aimultiple.com 'Benchmark Best 30 AI Governance Tools 2026'; atlan.com '7 Top AI Governance Tools for 2026'; centraleyes.com 'Top 13 AI Compliance Tools 2026'
reality_checks.differentiation_angle: The governance layer is well-served by Collibra, IBM, DataRobot, and cloud-native tools. BUT there is a narrow gap: none of these are SLM-SPECIFIC fine-tuning pipelines. They are governance overlays for general ML/AI. A platform that combines compliance-first SLM fine-tuning (data provenance verification BEFORE training, automated regulatory classification of training data, built-in PII/PHI scrubbing with audit proof, and sector-specific templates for HIPAA/SOX/EU AI Act) as a single integrated workflow - rather than bolting governance onto generic MLOps - could differentiate. Must be purpose-built for <10B parameter models in regulated verticals.

summary.most_contested_gap: MCP Supply Chain Security Scanner (Gap 14) - 12+ competitors found including Cisco and Snyk with corporate backing; 30+ CVEs already cataloged; multiple top-N lists exist; market is saturated
summary.least_contested_gap: Compliance-Aware Training Pipeline (Gap 15) - while AI governance is crowded, SLM-specific compliance-first fine-tuning remains underserved as a standalone integrated workflow
summary.key_finding: All 5 gaps have significant competition that was not reflected in original scores. The AI agent security and MCP security spaces have exploded with solutions in the last 6 months. The SLM training space has multiple well-funded platforms. No gap scored above 22.35 after reality adjustment, and none warrant a clean PROCEED verdict.
